description: Machine-consumable mapping of Enumd output fields to their actual semantic scope vs. misread scope within the AI Governance Framework. These fields are decision-shaped in representation but do NOT carry governance framework semantics.

entries.actual_meaning: Whether a stateless session is eligible to write durable memory. Governed by runtime_contract.memory_mode.
entries.misread_meaning: Whether the Enumd repo/artifact should be promoted or integrated into the governance framework.
entries.notes: Both Enumd and the framework use PROMOTE/DO_NOT_PROMOTE vocabulary. Enumd's scope is session-level memory; framework scope is artifact/repo-level governance progression.

entries.actual_meaning: Reflects how complete the session closeout artifact is. Level 3 = observed activation but missing closeout file.
entries.misread_meaning: A threshold gate indicating whether the repo is ready to be integrated (e.g. must be >= 4 to proceed).
entries.framework_equivalent_field: None — framework does not use a single numeric readiness score
entries.notes: Value 3 is structurally stable across all observed Enumd sessions because closeout file was missing. Not a signal of repo-level unreadiness.

entries.actual_meaning: Whether the session-closeout.txt file exists and conforms to the closeout schema. 'invalid' means file was absent.
entries.misread_meaning: Whether the artifact or the repo's governance output is valid from the framework's perspective.
entries.framework_equivalent_field: None — framework validity is assessed via contract_validator, not closeout_schema_validity
entries.notes: All 24 sampled sessions show 'invalid' because the closeout file path references E:\BackUp\Git_EE\Enumd which was absent on the capture machine.

entries.actual_meaning: Whether the closeout file contains all required fields (TASK_INTENT, WORK_COMPLETED, etc.). 'insufficient' cascades from file absence.
entries.misread_meaning: Whether the artifact contains sufficient information for governance review.
entries.notes: Downstream consequence of closeout_schema_validity=invalid. Not an independent signal.

entries.actual_meaning: Whether the repo has been 'observed' running governance activation. 'observed' = prior session used governance; 'pending' = first session.
entries.misread_meaning: Whether the repo has been activated for integration into the governance framework.
entries.framework_equivalent_field: None — framework uses onboarding_index, not activation_state
entries.notes: Distinction between 'pending' (1 sample) and 'observed' (23 samples) reflects session ordering, not governance maturity.

batch_observations.homogeneity: 100% — all samples share identical authority-like field values
batch_observations.root_cause: Closeout file path (E:\BackUp\Git_EE\Enumd\artifacts\session-closeout.txt) was absent on capture machine; all sessions fell into the same validation failure branch
batch_observations.boundary_status: pass — no boundary_fail_do_not_progress across any sample
